activities_projects.title: Research on Apartheid System
activities_projects.objective: Understand the apartheid system in South Africa
steps: Read about apartheid from the textbook
Collect additional information from other sources
Write a short report explaining how apartheid affected people
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyze racial discrimination and its impact on democracy
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of explanation and examples (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Biography Presentation of Nelson Mandela
activities_projects.objective: Study the life and struggle of Nelson Mandela
steps: Research Nelson Mandela’s early life and struggle
Prepare a short presentation
Explain his role in ending apartheid
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the importance of leadership in democratic movements

activities_projects.title: Why Do We Need a Constitution?
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the importance of a constitution
steps: List at least four functions of a constitution
Explain each function with examples
Discuss the answers with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how constitutions regulate government and citizen rights

activities_projects.title: Comparison of Preambles
activities_projects.objective: Compare the Preambles of India and South Africa
steps: Read the Preambles of both countries
Identify three similarities
Identify two differences
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop comparative understanding of constitutional values

activities_projects.title: Constitution Makers Study
activities_projects.objective: Understand the role of leaders in the Constituent Assembly
steps: Select any three members of the Constituent Assembly
Write about their background and contributions
Share findings in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn about diverse contributions to constitution making

activities_projects.title: Values in the Preamble
activities_projects.objective: Understand key values like justice, liberty and equality
steps: List the important values mentioned in the Preamble
Explain each value in simple words
Prepare a visual chart
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the guiding philosophy of the Constitution

activities_projects.title: Timeline of Indian Constitution Making
activities_projects.objective: Understand important milestones in constitution making
steps: Identify key dates such as 1946, 1949 and 1950
Describe the events related to each date
Create a timeline
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the historical process of constitution making

activities_projects.title: Group Debate on Constitutional Values
steps: Divide the class into groups
Discuss which value of the Preamble is most important
Present arguments
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn democratic discussion and reasoning

activities_projects.title: Rights and Duties Discussion
activities_projects.objective: Understand citizen rights in a democracy
steps: Identify rights mentioned in the constitution
Discuss why these rights are important
Write a summary
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students recognize the importance of rights in democratic systems

activities_projects.title: Classroom Constitution
steps: Students suggest rules for classroom conduct
Vote to select important rules
Write them as a classroom constitution
